results.caption:
They didn't just build ransomware. They built a brand.

DragonForce emerged December 2023 with a logo, a manifesto, and a business model that would net 363 victims across 30+ countries.

80% revenue share for affiliates. 20% for the cartel. Cleaner margins than most SaaS startups.

This is what organized cybercrime looks like in 2025.

#DragonForce #Ransomware #CyberSecurity #RaaS #ThreatIntelligence
results.hook: A mythological predator rendered in living circuitry stops every scroll — the face of the cartel that hit Marks & Spencer for £300M.

results.topic: The Binary Signature — Ransom Note Decoded
results.caption:
The ransom note isn't the end of the attack. It's the invoice.

DragonForce's encryption payload — ChaCha8 cross-platform — locks Windows, Linux, and ESXi environments simultaneously before the note even renders.

By the time your SOC reads it, the BYOVD drivers have already killed your EDR.

truesight.sys. rentdrv2.sys. Gone before the alert fires.

This is what the note looks like after forensics decode it.

#DragonForce #RansomNote #IncidentResponse #DFIR #CyberThreat
results.hook: A ransom note pinned under a forensic spotlight — with every line of extortion code dissected in real-time — makes the abstract horror of ransomware viscerally real.

results.caption:
Nobody knows exactly where DragonForce operates from.

Malaysia? Eastern Europe? A distributed network of affiliates with no fixed home?

The 'cartel' model was designed for this — maximum operational security through maximum decentralization.

Affiliate operators keep 80%. The core stays invisible.

Attribution is the hardest problem in cybersecurity. DragonForce was engineered to make it harder.

#ThreatIntelligence #Attribution #DragonForce #CyberEspionage #InfoSec
results.hook: A map with no confirmed X marks — the shadowed geography of an unattributed threat actor — provokes the professional paranoia every security leader feels.

results.caption:
December 6, 2023. A new post appeared on BreachForums.

Subject: DragonForce — New RaaS Program. Join Us.

Within 12 months: 200+ victims.
Within 24 months: 363 victims, 30+ countries, a 212.5% surge in attacks.

Every criminal empire has a founding document. This was theirs.

The dark web doesn't announce itself. It just opens for business.

#BreachForums #DarkWeb #DragonForce #RaaS #CyberCrime
results.hook: The exact moment a new criminal enterprise announced itself to the world — frozen as a forensic artifact — gives cybersecurity professionals the chilling timeline they need to share.

results.caption:
March 19, 2025. DragonForce didn't just announce an update.

They announced a cartel.

Three weeks later — April 8, 2025 — RansomHub was absorbed into their infrastructure.

Then came the UK campaign: Marks & Spencer, Co-op, Harrods. £300M+ in estimated impact. Peak activity: 35 organizations hit in December 2025 alone.

This is what consolidation of criminal power looks like in the ransomware economy.

When your threat actor starts issuing press releases, the threat level just changed.

#DragonForce #RansomHub #CartelAnnouncement #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence
results.hook: The moment a ransomware group publicly declared itself a cartel — absorbing RansomHub and reshaping the entire RaaS landscape — is the power move that demands a visual reckoning.

results.caption:
DragonForce didn't build a hacker group. They built a franchise.

Affiliates get 80%. The cartel takes 20%.
All they supply is the malware, the infrastructure, and the brand.

This is the McDonald's model — applied to ransomware.

The cartel announced its RaaS program on March 19, 2025.
By April 8, it had absorbed RansomHub's entire affiliate network.

This is what industrialized cybercrime looks like.

#Ransomware #DragonForce #RaaS #Cybersecurity #ThreatIntelligence
results.hook: The franchise model visual exposes how ransomware became a scalable business — shocking professionals who thought cybercrime was disorganized.

results.caption:
363 organizations. 212.5% surge in attacks. 35 victims in a single month.

DragonForce didn't scale slowly.
It exploded.

From its BreachForums debut in December 2023 to its peak in December 2025 — this cartel became one of the fastest-growing ransomware operations ever recorded.

The numbers aren't just statistics. They're businesses that stopped operating. Employees who couldn't get paid. Patients whose records were held hostage.

When was the last time your defenses were tested?

#DragonForce #Ransomware #CyberThreat #IncidentResponse #Cybersecurity
results.hook: A physical scoreboard counting real victims makes the abstract threat visceral and shareable — turning statistics into a gut-punch.

results.caption:
DragonForce didn't pick one target. It picked the world.

363 victims. 30+ countries. Every major English-speaking economy in the crosshairs.

US: 52% of all victims.
UK: 12% — including Marks & Spencer, Co-op, Harrods.
Australia: 6%.

The Summer 2025 UK retail attacks alone caused £300M+ in estimated impact.

No sector is immune. No geography is safe.

Is your supply chain as secure as your perimeter?

#DragonForce #GlobalThreat #Ransomware #UKCyber #CriticalInfrastructure
results.hook: A war-table map lit with attack vectors across continents makes geopolitical cyber threat feel immediate — driving shares from security professionals worldwide.

results.caption:
They didn't hack past your EDR.
They killed it using your own kernel's trust.

Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver — BYOVD.

DragonForce deploys truesight.sys and rentdrv2.sys — legitimate, signed Windows drivers — to terminate EDR processes directly from kernel space.

No exploit needed. Your OS trusts the driver. The driver kills your defenses. Then ChaCha8 encryption runs unopposed.

MITRE ATT&CK T1562.001 — Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools.

If your EDR dies before the ransomware runs, you will never see it coming.

#BYOVD #DragonForce #EDR #MITREATTACKFramework #RansomwareDefense
results.hook: Showing EDR tools physically 'dying' onscreen with real driver names and MITRE codes makes an abstract technique horrifyingly tangible for every security engineer.

results.caption:
No CVE. No zero-day. No sophisticated exploit.

Just a phone call.

DragonForce partnered with Scattered Spider — the social engineering collective responsible for MGM, Caesars, and the Summer 2025 UK retail attacks — to get inside before a single line of malicious code ran.

The technique: vishing. Voice phishing. A caller impersonating IT support convinces a help desk agent to reset MFA and hand over credentials.

The attacker is now inside. Legitimately. With your own credentials.

Now the ransomware runs.

Technology cannot defend against a voice. Only trained humans can.

#ScatteredSpider #SocialEngineering #Vishing #DragonForce #HumanRisk
results.hook: A phone call as the attack vector — not a keyboard — subverts every assumption security professionals have and demands an immediate share.

results.caption: For 9 days, DragonForce operators moved freely through the network. They mapped every server, every backup, every admin credential — all while EDR tools saw nothing. This is what BYOVD looks like from the inside. 363 organizations learned this lesson the hard way. Don't be next. #DragonForce #RansomwareDefense #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #ZeroTrust
results.hook: The haunting visual of an attacker living inside your network for 9 days unseen triggers every security professional's deepest fear.

results.caption: 6 terabytes. Payroll records. Customer data. Intellectual property. Board communications. All of it compressed, encrypted, and shipped out before a single alert fired. DragonForce affiliates used cloud staging to make exfiltration look like normal backup traffic. This is how 363 victims became leverage. #DataExfiltration #DragonForce #Ransomware #CyberThreat #IncidentResponse
results.hook: 6 terabytes vanishing silently into the dark web is a visceral number that makes every data owner stop scrolling.

results.caption: Before the encryption wave hits, DragonForce operators locate and destroy every backup. Shadow copies. Network shares. Cloud snapshots. Tape libraries. All of it — gone. This is why 'we have backups' is no longer a ransomware survival strategy. 363 victims. Many had backups. Most couldn't use them. #BackupSecurity #DragonForce #RansomwareResilience #IncidentResponse #CyberSecurity
results.hook: A burning tape library is the most visceral metaphor for the moment an organization realizes they have nothing left to recover from.

results.caption: ChaCha8. Cross-platform. Windows. Linux. ESXi. It doesn't matter what you run — DragonForce's encryption engine hits everything simultaneously. 212.5% surge in attacks. 35 organizations hit in a single December. The encryption wave doesn't wait for you to be ready. #ChaCha8 #DragonForce #RansomwareAsAService #CriticalInfrastructure #CyberAttack
results.hook: A tsunami of encrypted data rendered as a physical wave crashing over a skyline creates the kind of scale that makes the 212% attack surge tangible.

results.caption: After the encryption wave, DragonForce picks up the phone. Literally. Collaborating with Scattered Spider, they run vishing campaigns — calling employees, executives, IT staff. They already know your name. Your role. Your salary. They exfiltrated it 9 days ago. Affiliates keep 80%. They're highly motivated. The call is coming. #Vishing #ScatteredSpider #DragonForce #SocialEngineering #RansomwareResponse
results.hook: A ringing phone in a devastated executive office — the moment DragonForce calls to negotiate — is the human face of ransomware that stops every leader cold.
